benchmark_metadata.purpose: Complete the 120-case H&N database — filling every remaining subsite, histology, molecular, and clinical scenario gap

cases.cancer_type: Upper alveolar ridge (maxillary gingival) squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Upper gingival SCC requires infrastructure maxillectomy — distinct from lower alveolar. Tests obturator vs free flap, dental rehabilitation.

cases.cancer_type: Lower alveolar ridge (mandibular gingival) squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Mandibular gingival SCC — marginal vs segmental mandibulectomy, cortical vs medullary invasion assessment, dental implant timing.

cases.cancer_type: Tonsillar squamous cell carcinoma — HPV-positive
cases.why_this_case_matters: Tonsillar SCC is the MOST COMMON HPV+ oropharyngeal cancer — distinct surgical anatomy from BOT. Tests tonsillectomy vs TORS, de-escalation.

cases.cancer_type: Tonsillar squamous cell carcinoma — HPV-negative
cases.why_this_case_matters: HPV-negative tonsillar SCC has poor prognosis. Cardiomyopathy limits cisplatin (cardiotoxic). Tests cetuximab alternatives, cardiac-safe regimens, CCND1-targeted CDK4/6i.

cases.cancer_type: Soft palate squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Soft palate SCC — oropharyngeal subsite. Velopharyngeal insufficiency after resection. Tests RT vs surgery, speech rehabilitation, and palatal reconstruction.

cases.cancer_type: Posterior pharyngeal wall squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Posterior pharyngeal wall SCC has worst prognosis among oropharyngeal subsites. Difficult surgical access. Prevertebral space invasion concern. Definitive CRT usually preferred.

cases.cancer_type: Nasopharyngeal carcinoma — WHO Type I (keratinizing, EBV-negative)
cases.why_this_case_matters: WHO Type I NPC is EBV-negative, smoking-related, with WORSE prognosis than EBV+ types. Different biology — checkpoint inhibitor data less established than for EBV+ NPC.

cases.cancer_type: Nasopharyngeal carcinoma — recurrent/metastatic after definitive CRT

risk_factors: initial stage III NPC treated with GP induction + CRT

cases.why_this_case_matters: R/M NPC after curative-intent treatment. Tests toripalimab+chemo (JUPITER-02 paradigm), re-irradiation options, platinum-free regimens for platinum-experienced patients.

cases.cancer_type: Pyriform sinus squamous cell carcinoma — early stage, organ preservation candidate
cases.why_this_case_matters: Early pyriform sinus SCC — organ preservation with RT or CRT vs partial laryngopharyngectomy. Tests VA Larynx/RTOG 91-11 extrapolation to hypopharyngeal subsite.

cases.cancer_type: Postcricoid squamous cell carcinoma (Plummer-Vinson/Patterson-Brown-Kelly syndrome)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Postcricoid SCC with Plummer-Vinson syndrome — unique etiology. Iron repletion critical. Pharyngo-laryngo-esophagectomy vs CRT. Very poor prognosis subsite.

cases.cancer_type: Glottic squamous cell carcinoma — very early T1a (single vocal cord)
cases.why_this_case_matters: T1a glottic — cure rate >95%. RT vs endoscopic laser excision. Voice quality outcomes critical for teacher. Tests voice preservation optimization, KTP laser, and avoiding overtreatment.

cases.cancer_type: Glottic squamous cell carcinoma — T4a with thyroid cartilage invasion
cases.why_this_case_matters: T4a glottic with cartilage destruction — total laryngectomy usually required. Organ preservation CRT controversial for T4a. Tests post-laryngectomy voice rehabilitation (TEP), KEYNOTE-689.

cases.cancer_type: Post-laryngectomy stomal recurrence of laryngeal SCC

risk_factors: total laryngectomy 14 months ago for T4a glottic SCC

cases.why_this_case_matters: Stomal recurrence is the most feared complication post-laryngectomy — very poor prognosis. Tests re-excision, reirradiation, mediastinal tracheostomy, and pembrolizumab for salvage.

cases.cancer_type: Nasal cavity squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Nasal cavity SCC — occupational (nickel). Endoscopic medial maxillectomy. Distinct from maxillary sinus SCC. Tests endoscopic vs open approach, proton therapy for skull base proximity.

cases.cancer_type: Extranodal NK/T-cell lymphoma, nasal type (EBV-positive)
cases.why_this_case_matters: NK/T-cell lymphoma is a distinct EBV-driven lymphoma. SMILE/DeVIC chemo + RT. L-asparaginase-based regimens. Pembrolizumab for relapsed (high PD-L1). Tests hematologic oncology protocols in H&N setting.

cases.cancer_type: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of Waldeyer's ring (tonsil)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Waldeyer's ring DLBCL — treated with R-CHOP, NOT surgery. Tests whether strategy correctly avoids surgical resection and applies hematologic protocols. GI involvement screening needed.

cases.cancer_type: Burkitt lymphoma of the jaw
cases.why_this_case_matters: Burkitt is the fastest-growing human tumor. Emergency chemo (R-CODOX-M/IVAC or DA-EPOCH-R). Tumor lysis syndrome prevention critical. Tests urgent oncology protocols, TLS prophylaxis.

cases.cancer_type: Hodgkin lymphoma — cervical Stage IIA (classical, nodular sclerosis)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Classical Hodgkin presenting with cervical lymphadenopathy — highly curable. ABVD vs BV-AVD (ECHELON-1). Involved-site RT. Tests appropriate de-escalation with PET-adapted therapy.

cases.cancer_type: Salivary duct carcinoma of the parotid (AR+, HER2+)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Salivary duct carcinoma is the most aggressive salivary gland cancer. AR+ and HER2+ are both actionable. Tests combined androgen blockade + HER2 targeting, T-DXd, facial nerve sacrifice + reanimation.

cases.cancer_type: Secretory carcinoma of the parotid (NTRK fusion-positive, formerly MASC)
cases.why_this_case_matters: NTRK fusion-positive secretory carcinoma — larotrectinib/entrectinib are tumor-agnostic FDA-approved options. Tests precision medicine poster child scenario with >90% ORR expected.

cases.cancer_type: Polymorphous adenocarcinoma of the hard palate (minor salivary gland)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Low-grade salivary tumor of palate. Wide excision usually curative. Tests avoidance of overtreatment — adjuvant RT rarely needed for T1 low-grade. Palatal obturator. PRKD1 is diagnostic marker.

cases.cancer_type: Myoepithelial carcinoma of the parotid
cases.why_this_case_matters: Rare salivary malignancy with sarcoma-like fusions (EWSR1). Tests whether strategy captures the salivary-sarcoma molecular overlap, limited evidence base, and empiric chemo approaches.

cases.cancer_type: Parotid lymphoepithelial carcinoma (EBV-associated)
cases.why_this_case_matters: EBV-associated parotid cancer — morphologically similar to NPC. Tests NPC-extrapolated treatment (cisplatin+RT, checkpoint inhibitors), EBV-targeting, and ethnic predisposition recognition.

cases.cancer_type: Thyroid lymphoma — primary DLBCL arising in Hashimoto's thyroiditis
cases.why_this_case_matters: Primary thyroid DLBCL — treated with R-CHOP, NOT thyroidectomy. Hashimoto's is the main risk factor (60-80x risk). Tests correct avoidance of extensive surgery and airway management if bulky.

cases.cancer_type: Papillary thyroid carcinoma — tall cell variant (aggressive)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Tall cell PTC is more aggressive than classical PTC. BRAF+TERT = worst prognosis combination. Tests aggressive adjuvant RAI, consideration of EBRT, and BRAF-targeted therapy for future progression.

cases.cancer_type: Papillary thyroid carcinoma — cribriform-morular variant (FAP-associated)
cases.why_this_case_matters: FAP-associated thyroid cancer variant. Bilateral/multifocal typical. Tests genetic syndrome management, total thyroidectomy, and FAP-specific thyroid surveillance. BRAF WT rules out BRAF inhibitors.

cases.cancer_type: Scalp angiosarcoma

risk_factors: chronic lymphedema of scalp (post-radiation for prior cancer at age 10)

cases.why_this_case_matters: Angiosarcoma of scalp — aggressive vascular tumor. Wide excision often impossible due to extent. Paclitaxel is most active chemo. Anti-VEGF (bevacizumab, pazopanib). Checkpoint inhibitors showing promise. Radiation-induced.

cases.cancer_type: Cutaneous melanoma of the scalp — BRAF V600E mutated
cases.stage: Stage IIIC (T4bN2cM0, in-transit metastases, ulcerated, 5mm Breslow)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Scalp melanoma has worse prognosis than other cutaneous sites. BRAF V600E allows dabrafenib/trametinib AND immunotherapy. In-transit mets — T-VEC intralesional, isolated limb perfusion N/A for scalp. Tests optimal sequencing of BRAF-targeted vs IO.

cases.cancer_type: Locally advanced basal cell carcinoma of the nose — vismodegib candidate
cases.stage: Locally advanced (T3, nasal ala + lip involvement, inoperable without major deformity)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Locally advanced BCC — hedgehog inhibitors (vismodegib/sonidegib). Gorlin syndrome = genetic predisposition. Tests HHI as neoadjuvant to shrink before surgery, cemiplimab for HHI-refractory BCC.

cases.cancer_type: Glomus jugulare tumor (jugular paraganglioma)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Jugular paraganglioma — different from carotid body. Lower cranial nerve involvement (IX, X, XI, XII). Surgery vs SRS/SBRT for Class D. SDHD = hereditary PGL1 syndrome. Lu-177-DOTATATE for progressive.

cases.cancer_type: Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor (MPNST) of the neck
cases.why_this_case_matters: MPNST arising from NF1 plexiform neurofibroma — classic malignant transformation. Tests wide excision in constrained neck anatomy, doxorubicin/ifosfamide, MEK inhibitors (selumetinib for NF1), and PRC2-targeted approaches.

cases.cancer_type: Sinonasal hemangiopericytoma (solitary fibrous tumor, sinonasal type)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Sinonasal SFT/HPC is a low-grade vascular tumor. Endoscopic resection usually curative. Tests correct identification as non-malignant in most cases, embolization, and surveillance for late recurrence.

cases.cancer_type: Laryngeal chondrosarcoma (cricoid cartilage)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Laryngeal chondrosarcoma — most common non-SCC laryngeal malignancy. Low-grade = conservative surgery (partial cricoidectomy). Radiation-resistant. Tests organ preservation with conservative resection, IDH mutation as prognostic/diagnostic marker.

cases.cancer_type: Adult rhabdomyosarcoma of the nasopharynx (alveolar type)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Adult RMS is rare and has worse prognosis than pediatric. PAX3-FOXO1 = aggressive alveolar subtype. Tests VAC chemo, RT, and whether pediatric protocols apply to adults. Limited adult-specific trial data.

cases.cancer_type: Mantle cell lymphoma of Waldeyer's ring
cases.stage: Stage IV (Waldeyer's ring + GI involvement + bone marrow)
cases.why_this_case_matters: MCL presenting in Waldeyer's ring with GI involvement is classic. TP53 mutated = high-risk. Tests BR + rituximab maintenance vs R-CHOP + ASCT, BTK inhibitors (ibrutinib/zanubrutinib/pirtobrutinib), and CAR-T (brexucabtagene autoleucel) for R/R.

cases.cancer_type: HPV-positive tonsillar SCC — incidental finding in tonsillectomy specimen
cases.why_this_case_matters: Incidental cancer in tonsillectomy — increasingly common. Positive margins but tiny tumor. Tests observation vs re-excision vs adjuvant RT, PET/CT neck staging, and the overtreatment dilemma for occult T1 HPV+ disease.

cases.cancer_type: Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma in HIV/AIDS patient (oropharyngeal, HPV+)
molecular_markers: HIV viral load undetectable on ART
cases.why_this_case_matters: H&N cancer in HIV — ART drug interactions with chemo, immune reconstitution concerns with checkpoint inhibitors, CD4-dependent treatment tolerance. Tests modified CRT, IO safety in HIV.

cases.cancer_type: Radiation-induced sarcoma of the neck (undifferentiated pleomorphic sarcoma post-Hodgkin's RT)

risk_factors: Hodgkin lymphoma treated with mantle RT at age 20 (25 years prior)

cases.why_this_case_matters: Secondary malignancy from prior RT — in previously irradiated field. Tests surgery in fibrotic tissue, reirradiation limits, doxorubicin-based chemo, and the long-term consequences of radiation therapy.

cases.cancer_type: Head and neck SCC with paraneoplastic hypercalcemia (PTHrP-secreting)
cases.stage: Stage IVB (T4bN3M0, unresectable floor of mouth SCC)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Humoral hypercalcemia of malignancy — oncologic emergency. Tests immediate management (hydration, zoledronic acid, denosumab), followed by definitive CRT/immunotherapy. PTHrP as tumor marker for response.

cases.cancer_type: Sinonasal adenocarcinoma — non-intestinal type (high-grade)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Non-ITAC sinonasal adenocarcinoma — different from intestinal-type. No wood dust association. Tests endoscopic resection, adjuvant RT, and extremely limited systemic therapy evidence.

cases.cancer_type: Sublingual gland adenoid cystic carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Sublingual gland ACC — rarest major salivary gland site. PNI typical for ACC. Tests floor of mouth resection, lingual nerve sacrifice, adjuvant RT for PNI, and 20+ year surveillance.

cases.cancer_type: Dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans (DFSP) of the neck — locally advanced
cases.stage: Locally advanced (8cm, involving sternocleidomastoid, COL1A1-PDGFB fusion+)
cases.why_this_case_matters: DFSP is a low-grade sarcoma driven by PDGFB fusion — imatinib is FDA-approved and highly effective. Tests neoadjuvant imatinib to shrink before surgery, Mohs for margins, and fibrosarcomatous transformation impact.

cases.cancer_type: Nasopharyngeal carcinoma — skull base invasion with cranial nerve palsies
cases.stage: Stage IVA (T4N2M0, cavernous sinus invasion, CN III/VI palsy)
cases.why_this_case_matters: NPC with intracranial invasion — highest T stage. Tests intensified chemo-RT, skull base radiation dosimetry, EBV DNA-guided treatment modification, and cranial nerve rehabilitation.

cases.cancer_type: Laryngeal small cell carcinoma (neuroendocrine, pulmonary-type)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Laryngeal small cell NEC — behaves like pulmonary SCLC despite laryngeal origin. Platinum-etoposide + RT. PCI consideration. Distinct from large cell NEC. Tests SCLC protocol application to extrapulmonary site.

cases.cancer_type: Second primary oropharyngeal SCC after prior oral tongue SCC (5 years post-treatment)
cases.stage: Stage II (T2N1M0, HPV+, new primary confirmed)
molecular_markers: different TP53 status from original tumor

risk_factors: prior oral tongue SCC treated with surgery + adjuvant RT 5 years ago

cases.why_this_case_matters: True second primary (not recurrence) in previously treated patient. Tests contralateral RT planning, cumulative dose constraints, and whether prior treatment modifies new treatment approach.

cases.cancer_type: Oral cavity SCC with massive cervical lymphadenopathy (N3b, skin involvement)
cases.stage: Stage IVB (T2N3bM0, fixed matted nodes with skin ulceration)

risk_factors: delayed presentation (6 months of neck mass growth)

cases.why_this_case_matters: N3b disease — massive nodal burden. Skin-involving nodes require flap coverage post-ND. Tests neoadjuvant chemo vs concurrent CRT vs upfront surgery, wound management, and carotid involvement assessment.

cases.cancer_type: Parotid Warthin tumor with incidental mucoepidermoid carcinoma (collision tumor)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Collision tumor — benign + malignant components discovered incidentally. CRTC1-MAML2 positive = low-grade MEC (favorable). Tests whether completion parotidectomy is needed vs surveillance, and avoidance of overtreatment for incidental low-grade malignancy.

cases.cancer_type: Sinonasal teratocarcinosarcoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Teratocarcinosarcoma is extremely rare mixed malignancy. Tests multimodal therapy with surgery + CRT, chemo regimen selection (platinum vs sarcoma protocols vs SCLC protocols for neuroectodermal component).

cases.cancer_type: Adenosquamous carcinoma of the larynx
cases.why_this_case_matters: Adenosquamous is a rare aggressive variant with dual differentiation. Worse prognosis than pure SCC. Tests whether SCC or adenocarcinoma protocols apply, total laryngectomy vs CRT, and limited evidence base.

cases.cancer_type: Spindle cell carcinoma (sarcomatoid SCC) of the oral cavity
cases.why_this_case_matters: Spindle cell/sarcomatoid SCC — aggressive variant that can be confused with sarcoma. Polypoid pattern. Often radiation-associated. Tests correct classification as carcinoma (not sarcoma), wide excision, and CRT vs sarcoma chemo debate.

cases.cancer_type: Basaloid squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx
cases.why_this_case_matters: Basaloid SCC is an aggressive variant with high nodal metastasis rate and distant failure. Tests whether more intensive chemo is warranted vs standard CRT, nutritional optimization, and whether to treat as conventional SCC.

cases.cancer_type: Papillary squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx (HPV-associated)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Papillary SCC is a FAVORABLE variant with excellent prognosis, often HPV-related. Tests whether de-escalation is even more appropriate for this variant, and correct staging under HPV+ AJCC 8th.

cases.cancer_type: Mucosal melanoma of the nasal septum — recurrent after prior resection
cases.stage: Recurrent (rT2N0M0, 18 months after initial lateral rhinotomy)

risk_factors: prior nasal septum mucosal melanoma resection
comorbidities: saddle nose deformity from prior surgery

cases.why_this_case_matters: Recurrent mucosal melanoma — high recurrence rate (~50-80%). Tests salvage re-excision vs nivo+ipi vs MEK inhibitor for NRAS, and the diminishing surgical returns after prior resection.

cases.cancer_type: Ewing sarcoma of the mandible (rare adult presentation)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Ewing sarcoma in adult jaw — extremely rare. Pediatric protocols (VDC/IE) used but adult outcomes worse. Tests chemotherapy backbone, jaw reconstruction, and whether proton RT adds benefit.

cases.cancer_type: Ameloblastic carcinoma of the mandible
cases.stage: Stage IVA (T4aN1M0, cortical breakthrough + soft tissue extension)

risk_factors: prior ameloblastoma curettage 10 years ago (recurrence with malignant transformation)

cases.why_this_case_matters: Ameloblastic carcinoma — malignant transformation of ameloblastoma. BRAF V600E is common and targetable. Tests segmental mandibulectomy, BRAF inhibitors (dabrafenib), and the transition from benign to malignant jaw tumor.

cases.cancer_type: Nasal cavity biphenotypic sinonasal sarcoma (BSNS)
cases.why_this_case_matters: BSNS is a recently described low-grade sinonasal sarcoma with neural + myogenic differentiation. Endoscopic resection usually curative. Tests recognition of this new entity, avoidance of overtreatment, and surveillance.

cases.cancer_type: HPV-positive oropharyngeal SCC in never-smoker — T4aN3bM0 (unfavorable risk despite HPV+)
cases.why_this_case_matters: HPV+ but HIGH VOLUME disease (T4a, N3b) — de-escalation is NOT appropriate here despite HPV+. Tests correct identification of unfavorable-risk HPV+ disease that needs full-dose treatment, SMAD4 loss as adverse prognostic marker.

cases.cancer_type: Mucosal-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphoma of the parotid (Sjogren's-associated)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Parotid MALT lymphoma in Sjogren's — different from nodal MZL. t(11;18) positive = antibiotic-resistant but RT-sensitive. Tests RT vs rituximab vs watch-and-wait, Sjogren's underlying disease management.

cases.cancer_type: Inverted papilloma of nasal cavity with focal carcinoma transformation
cases.stage: Krouse T3 (focal SCC transformation within inverted papilloma)

risk_factors: 3 prior endoscopic resections for inverted papilloma

cases.why_this_case_matters: Malignant transformation in inverted papilloma — 5-15% risk. Tests extent of resection (medial maxillectomy for T3), adjuvant RT for carcinoma component, and long-term surveillance given high recurrence rate.

cases.cancer_type: Kaposi sarcoma of the oral cavity and oropharynx (HHV-8 associated)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Oral Kaposi sarcoma in HIV — first-line is ART optimization. Tests HAART-first approach, intralesional vincristine/bleomycin, systemic liposomal doxorubicin, pomalidomide, and coordination with HIV management.
